items.headline: Think AI Spending Won’t Stoke Inflation? This Time Could Be Different.
items.summary: Big tech spending on artificial intelligence is set to soar past $1 trillion next year as companies race to secure their place in the queue of the world’s hottest technology.  The upshot of all that spending, however, is a likely boost to inflation prospects, which are already rising from oil and energy prices tied to the U.S. war with Iran and the on again, off again nature of tariffs put in place by President Donald Trump.  “There is a regime shift underway in technology goods inflation,” said  Stifel  research analyst Thomas Carroll, who notes that 2026 “marks the first time in 65 years that tech goods prices are rising faster than wages.”

items.headline: What is Jevons Paradox and why does more AI mean more labor?
items.summary: Jevons Paradox outlines that increased efficiency in resource usage often leads to higher — rather than lower — total consumption. So what does that mean for the big push into AI for industries outside of Big Tech? Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor Josh Schafer relate this economic theory to the AI Boom and what the pivot toward the "everything is AI now" philosophy has meant for AI consumption and labor demands.
